title: dyera polyphylla
intro: Dyera polyphylla, also known as the Canarium tree or Agarwood, is a tropical plant species native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia. Its bark and wood contain high concentrations of agarwood resin, a resinous substance with medicinal properties, which is believed to have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dyera polyphylla include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as tea, powder, and essential oils, which can be consumed or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
